title: Penstemon angelicus
intro: Penstemon angelicus, commonly known as White Penstemon or Angel's Beard, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the western United States and Canada, belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Penstemon angelicus include iridoid glycosides, such as penstemonoside and ajugoside, as well as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and to support immune system function.
taxonomy: Penstemon angelicus is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a type of beardtongue, which is a common name for plants in the Penstemon genus. This genus is a member of the tribe Veroniceae, which is a group of plants within the order Lamiales. Penstemon angelicus is native to the western United States and is also known as the White Penstemon. It is an herbaceous perennial plant that grows in dry to moist areas with well-drained soil.
uses: Penstemon angelicus, also known as White Penstemon, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have been used by Native American tribes to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and wounds.
